tool_args.label: Proposal to integrate Academy Bus and Coach USA into MTA
tool_args.raw_text: proposal for the acquisition and integration of private commuter bus services, specifically Academy Bus Lines' commuter division and Coach USA's routes (under Renco Group), into a unified MTA Express Bus network under the New York City Transit Authority (NYCTA)

payload.claim_text: A proposal was submitted to the MTA to acquire Academy Bus Lines' commuter division, Coach USA's routes (under Renco Group), and Private Transportation Corporation (B110), and integrate them along with MTA Bus Company into a unified NYCTA express bus network.
payload.description: Public hearing comment proposing integration of private commuter bus services into MTA

evidence_refs.source_quote: propose a comprehensive strategy for the acquisition and integration of private commuter bus services, specifically Academy Bus Lines' commuter division and Coach USA's routes (under Renco Group), into a unified MTA Express Bus network under the New York City Transit Authority (NYCTA)

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Proposal for OMNY transition deadline July 2026
tool_args.raw_text: proposal that the MTA formally cease acceptance of all MetroCards and onboard bus coin payment effective July 1, 2026, at 12:00 AM

payload.claim_text: A proposal was submitted to the MTA to cease acceptance of all MetroCards and onboard bus coin payment effective July 1, 2026, as part of the MetroCard-to-OMNY transition.
payload.description: Public hearing comment proposing an OMNY system sunset date

evidence_refs.source_quote: I propose that the MTA formally cease acceptance of all MetroCards and onboard bus coin payment effective July 1, 2026, at 12:00 AM

tool_args.label: Proposal to deploy R211 on B and D subway lines
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA should begin deploying the R211A on the D line in 2026... have both B and D lines combined to operate exclusively R211A subway cars by 2027

payload.claim_text: A proposal was submitted recommending R211A deployment on the D line in 2026, with both B and D lines operating exclusively R211A subway cars by July 1, 2027, phasing out R68 and R68A cars.
payload.description: Public hearing comment proposing R211 subway car deployment on B and D lines

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA should begin deploying the R211A on the D line in 2026. This would allow the MTA to deploy a CBTC equipped fleet in the rolling stock onto a subway line that is slated to receive CBTC in the future

tool_args.label: 59th Street Junction deinterlining proposal
tool_args.raw_text: deinterlining of the 59th Street Junction... A train re-routed to 168th Street, B train to Inwood-207th Street, C train to Bedford Park Boulevard, D train remains at Norwood-205th Street

payload.claim_text: A proposal was submitted to deinterline the 59th Street Junction by re-routing the A train to 168th Street, B train to Inwood-207th Street, C train to Bedford Park Boulevard, with the D train maintaining its route to Norwood-205th Street. Target implementation: January 2028.
payload.description: Public hearing comment proposing deinterlining of the 59th Street Junction

evidence_refs.source_quote: the A train would be re-routed to 168th Street, effectively replacing the C train on that segment. The B train would be re-routed to Inwood-207th Street, replacing the A train's current terminal. Concurrently, the C train would be re-routed to Bedford Park Boulevard, taking over the segment currently served by the B train during rush hours. The D train would maintain its current route to Norwood-205th Street

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: DeKalb Junction deinterlining proposal
tool_args.raw_text: deinterlining proposal for DeKalb Junction... B train re-routed to Bay Ridge-96th Street, D/N/Q maintain current routes, R train re-routed to Brighton Beach

payload.claim_text: A proposal was submitted to deinterline the DeKalb Junction by re-routing the B train to Bay Ridge-96th Street, keeping D/N/Q trains on their current routes, and re-routing the R train to Brighton Beach. Target implementation: Summer 2027.
payload.description: Public hearing comment proposing deinterlining of the DeKalb Junction

evidence_refs.source_quote: the B train would be re-routed to Bay Ridge-96th Street, effectively replacing the R train on that segment. The D, N, and Q trains would maintain their current routes to Coney Island-Stillwell Avenue... the R train would be re-routed to Brighton Beach, taking over the segment currently served by the B train

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Hydrogen fuel-cell bus proposal
tool_args.raw_text: proposal for integrating hydrogen fuel-cell buses into the MTA's 2040 zero-emissions fleet strategy alongside battery-electric buses

payload.claim_text: A proposal was submitted to integrate hydrogen fuel-cell buses into the MTA's 2040 zero-emission fleet strategy. Proposed depots: Jackie Gleason (Brooklyn), West Farms (Bronx), Manhattanville (Manhattan), College Point (Queens), and Yonkers (Staten Island). Proposed procurement of 800 hydrogen fuel-cell buses.
payload.description: Public hearing comment proposing hydrogen fuel-cell bus integration into MTA fleet

evidence_refs.source_quote: proposal for integrating hydrogen fuel-cell buses into the MTA's 2040 zero-emissions fleet strategy alongside battery-electric buses

tool_args.label: Suffolk County LIRR modernization call
tool_args.raw_text: Suffolk County requires a revitalized 21st-century rail system and modernization of rail service

payload.claim_text: Suffolk County Executive Edward Romaine submitted a comment calling for comprehensive modernization of the LIRR in Suffolk County through strategic infrastructure investment in double-tracking and electrification, noting that more than 73% of the County's land area and approximately 50% of its residents rely on diesel rail service.
payload.description: Public hearing comment from Suffolk County Executive urging LIRR modernization

evidence_refs.source_quote: I am calling for a comprehensive modernization of the LIRR in Suffolk County through strategic infrastructure investment in double-tracking and electrification

The 125th St-2nd Av stop is proposed to serve the 2nd Av/Queens Blvd (U) line

payload.claim_text: Jesse Figueras, a US Army Veteran and Accessibility and Transportation Advocate from Bay Ridge, Brooklyn, submitted a public comment expressing strong support for the MTA's proposed federal grant projects for FFY 2026, specifically highlighting $3.28 billion for passenger stations, $12.58 billion for New York City Transit, and the need for State of Good Repair and signal upgrades.
